components.content: CLI command for viewing developer profile from ~/.rai/developer.yaml.
Display the developer profile in YAML format.
CLI command for viewing bundled base Rai package information.
Display base Rai package information.
Abstract interface for backlog providers (JIRA, GitLab, Odoo, Local). Defines the contract for bidirectional sync operations with provider-agnostic methods.
JIRA implementation of BacklogProvider interface. Provides type-safe methods for reading epics and stories with rate limiting, field filtering, and OAuth token integration.
Token bucket rate limiter for API calls. Enforces maximum request rate to comply with JIRA Cloud limits (10 req/sec) using sliding window algorithm.
JIRA Epic with filtered fields only (key, summary, description, status, labels). Used for efficient API responses.
JIRA Story/Issue with filtered fields only (key, summary, description, status, labels, epic_key). Used for efficient API responses.
Data required to create a JIRA story (summary, description, labels). Input model for story creation operations.
RaiSE sync metadata for JIRA entity properties (ADR-028). Tracks what's been synced, when, and by whom. Includes epic_id, story_id, task_id, timestamps, branch info, and conflict detection fields.
JIRA entity property wrapper for RaiSE sync metadata (ADR-028). Wraps RaiSyncMetadata in the structure expected by JIRA entity properties API.
Local story data for push to JIRA. Contains story metadata for sync operations.
Result of a pull operation from JIRA. Contains import summary with epic and story counts.
Result of a push operation to JIRA. Contains creation summary with created and skipped story counts.
Result of an authorization check. Indicates whether a story is authorized to work on based on JIRA status.
Mapping between a local RaiSE ID and a JIRA issue key. Tracks sync relationship for a single epic or story with status and timestamps.
Persistent sync state between RaiSE and JIRA. Stored in .raise/rai/sync/state.json. Contains provider info, cloud_id, project_key, and epic/story mappings.
Base exception for JIRA operations. All JIRA-specific exceptions inherit from this class.
JIRA API error with HTTP status code. Raised when JIRA REST API returns an error response.
Authentication or authorization failed. Raised for invalid/expired OAuth tokens, permission issues, or HTTP 401/403 responses.
Requested resource not found. Raised when epic or story key doesn't exist or user lacks permission (HTTP 404).
Rate limit exceeded. Raised when server-side rate limit hit (HTTP 429). Client-side rate limiting should prevent this in normal operation.
OAuth flow error. Raised when any step of the OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code + PKCE flow fails.
Complete OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code + PKCE flow for JIRA. Orchestrates authorization URL, browser redirect, callback server, token exchange, and credential storage.

metadata.name: authenticate
metadata.signature: def authenticate(client_id: str, client_secret: str, redirect_uri: str='http://localhost:8080/callback', credentials_path: Path | None=None, scopes: list[str] | None=None) -> dict[str, Any]
